presets.reply: Hello! I'm your clinical AI assistant, focused on medical record documentation, clinical Q&A, and literature search. How can I help with your medical questions today?

For the Prolanis follow-up case (Tn. AS, 58 y/o): today's labs show HbA1c 7.4% (rising from 7.1%), BP 148/92 mmHg, and urinalysis protein +1 suggesting early DKD. Primary coding: E11.9 with candidate upgrade to E11.21 pending UACR. Would you like to open Ambient AI for live note capture, or run Insurance Coding?

I can generate an INA-CBG / BPJS coding report from a clinical record. Available presets: Prolanis Note (E11.9 + I10 + E78.2 + E66.3) and TB Discharge (A15.0 + E11.9 + D64.9 with ICD-9-CM procedures). Click Insurance Assistant below or type "run insurance coding" to start.

I can run a Clinical Decision Support review for drug interactions. The geriatric case (Ny. KW, 72 y/o, 9 medications) flags severe alerts: Glibenclamide + eGFR 48 (Beers Criteria), triple whammy NSAID+ACE-I+diuretic, and off-label Tamsulosin. Click Clinical Assistant to run the full analysis.

Ambient AI can capture the Prolanis consultation live — mixed Bahasa/English transcription streams on the left while a structured SOAP note generates on the right. Click AI Writing to launch the outpatient record demo.

The TB discharge summary case includes primary diagnosis A15.0 (sputum-confirmed pulmonary TB), secondary E11.9 and D64.9, plus procedures 87.42, 87.44, and 89.07 for INA-CBGs Group A-4-12-II. Select TB Discharge in Insurance Coding to view the full report.

defaultReply: I'm your Cognitas clinical AI assistant. I can help with ambient documentation (AI Writing), insurance coding (ICD-10 / ICD-9-CM), and clinical decision support for drug interactions. Try asking about Prolanis, insurance coding, or medication review — or use the quick-action tags below.
